region_of_origin: Netherlands (Nutricia/Danone Specialized Nutrition); distributed in Singapore
food_subgroup: FSMP — Foods for Special Medical Purposes (SFA Singapore). Indication: High protein requirements with severe volume intolerance (oncology, post-surgical, geriatric, dysphagia patients).

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Fortisip Compact Protein as a compact-format FSMP oral nutritional supplement. No exact or close FoodOn term available as of 2026.
ontology.foodex2_description: Oral nutritional supplement, compact, high protein, liquid (closest parent match)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0QEL used as closest parent for compact high-protein liquid FSMP; no exact FoodEx2 term for Fortisip Compact Protein format.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229949006 is the closest available term for a food supplement preparation; no specific SNOMED CT code exists for Fortisip Compact Protein.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one 125ml bottle (approximately 130g), providing 300 kcal, 18g protein, and 176mg sodium — the smallest-volume high-protein oral nutritional supplement available in Singapore hospitals.

nutrient_source.title: Fortisip Compact Protein Vanilla — Nutricia Singapore Product Sheet (Official Manufacturer Nutrition Label)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Nutricia Singapore official product sheet for Fortisip Compact Protein Vanilla 125ml. This is a manufactured FSMP with a fixed, declared nutritional composition. Per-100g values are derived by scaling from the per-125ml label values (÷1.25 for per-100ml, then adjusted for density ~1.04g/ml to yield per-100g). Values are consistent with the manufacturer's declared nutrition panel. As a registered FSMP under SFA Singapore, the declared values are regulatory-grade. USDA FoodData Central does not contain an entry for this specific product; manufacturer label data is the appropriate primary source for branded FSMP products per Project Veritas source policy (USDA Branded Food equivalent).

gi_source.title: Glycemic index estimated from Nutricia published data for Fortisip Compact Protein; analogous to low-GI high-protein liquid oral supplements documented in peer-reviewed literature (FOS + milk protein moderating glucose absorption)
gi_source.organization: Nutricia (manufacturer published data); supported by University of Sydney GI Database analogous liquid nutritional supplement category
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI ~35 is based on Nutricia published data for Fortisip Compact Protein Vanilla. The University of Sydney GI Database documents similar high-protein, FOS-containing liquid oral supplements in the GI 30–40 range. The combination of milk protein concentrate, FOS (prebiotic fibre), and moderate sucrose content with high protein density produces a low GI response. GL per 125ml bottle = (35 × 32g carbohydrate) / 100 = 11.2. Confidence: 0.9 (referenced, manufacturer-supported, analogous GI database corroboration).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content: 132mg per 100g, 176mg per 125ml bottle
hypertension.guidance: Fortisip Compact Protein Vanilla contains only 176mg sodium per 125ml bottle, making it an appropriate oral nutritional supplement for hypertensive patients with malnutrition or high protein requirements; no sodium restriction adjustment is needed at standard one-bottle dosing.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~35) and moderate glycemic load (~11 per bottle) due to FOS and high milk protein content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 35 and GL of ~11 per 125ml bottle, Fortisip Compact Protein Vanilla is an acceptable oral nutritional supplement for T2DM patients with severe volume intolerance or oncology-related malnutrition; blood glucose monitoring is recommended, particularly in frail elderly or post-surgical T2DM patients.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.9g per 100g, 1.2g per bottle), zero trans fat, and low sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Fortisip Compact Protein Vanilla has a favourable cardiovascular profile with low saturated fat (1.2g per bottle), zero trans fat, and low sodium (176mg per bottle), making it suitable for cardiovascular disease patients requiring high-protein nutritional support in small volumes.

chronic_kidney_disease.key_factor: Elevated potassium (300.8mg per 100g, ~391mg per bottle) and phosphorus (125mg per 100g, ~163mg per bottle)
chronic_kidney_disease.guidance: CKD patients on potassium or phosphorus restriction should use Fortisip Compact Protein Vanilla with caution and under dietitian supervision, as each bottle provides approximately 391mg potassium and 163mg phosphorus.

dysphagia.key_factor: Small 125ml volume with high caloric and protein density suitable for patients with swallowing difficulties
dysphagia.guidance: Fortisip Compact Protein Vanilla is the preferred oral supplement for dysphagia patients requiring high protein and caloric density in minimal volume; consult speech therapist for appropriate texture/viscosity assessment.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fortisip Compact Protein Vanilla (Nutricia/Danone) is a ready-to-drink, ultra-high-density oral nutritional supplement (ONS) classified as a Food for Special Medical Purposes (FSMP) under SFA Singapore regulations. Each 125ml bottle delivers 300 kcal (2.4 kcal/ml), 18g protein, 11.6g fat (1.2g saturated fat, 0g trans fat), 32g carbohydrate, 176mg sodium, and 400mg potassium. Per 100g: 225.6 kcal, 13.5g protein, 8.7g fat, 24.1g carbohydrate, 1.5g dietary fibre, 132mg sodium. It is the most calorie-dense standard ONS available in Singapore hospitals — providing equivalent calories to a 237ml Ensure Plus in just 125ml. The vanilla-flavoured liquid has a smooth, creamy texture and mild sweetness from sucrose and vanilla flavouring, with fructooligosaccharides (FOS) contributing to its low glycemic index (~35) and prebiotic benefit. Glycemic load per bottle is approximately 11 (low). Primary clinical indications include: severe volume intolerance (fluid-restricted patients, dysphagia, post-surgical ileus recovery), oncology-related malnutrition, frail elderly with early satiety, and post-surgical high-protein requirements. It is halal-certified, gluten-free, and vegetarian. Registered and distributed by Nutricia Singapore (Danone Specialized Nutrition) and used in Singapore geriatric medicine (TTSH, SGH), oncology (NCIS, Sengkang General Hospital), and post-surgical wards. Monitor potassium and phosphorus in CKD patients. Confidence: HIGH — manufacturer-declared FSMP nutrition panel.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the Nutricia Singapore official product sheet for Fortisip Compact Protein Vanilla 125ml, a regulatory-grade FSMP nutrition declaration under SFA Singapore. All core macros, secondary nutrients, and micronutrients are manufacturer-declared with fixed formulation. GI is manufacturer-referenced and corroborated by University of Sydney GI Database analogous liquid supplement category. Confidence: HIGH.
notes: Per-100g values are derived by scaling from the per-125ml manufacturer label (300 kcal, 18g protein, 11.6g fat, 32g carbohydrate, 1.2g saturated fat, 0g trans fat, 16g sugar, 2g dietary fibre, 176mg sodium, 400mg potassium, 200mg calcium, 53mg magnesium, 3.3mg iron, 6.5mg cholesterol, 1.2g saturated fat) divided by 1.25 (volume) and adjusted for density (~1.04g/ml) to yield per-100g values consistent with the input record. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not published in the Nutricia Singapore product sheet or SFA FSMP registration data as of 2026-04-14. water_g is set to -1: moisture content not declared on the Nutricia Singapore product sheet. trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 per manufacturer declaration. All -1 fields: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g — data not available in Nutricia Singapore product sheet or SFA FSMP database as of April 2026. GI ~35 is an analogous estimate supported by Nutricia published data and University of Sydney GI Database liquid supplement category; confidence 0.9. This product is an FSMP and should only be used under medical or dietitian supervision. CKD patients should monitor potassium (~391mg/bottle) and phosphorus (~163mg/bottle). The food_name has been shortened from the verbose input name to a canonical form suitable for database use.
